vision.title: Vision & Mission
vision.subtitle: National transportation policy direction of Indonesia
vision.vision_text: To realize a national transportation system that is safe, orderly, smooth, sustainable, and globally competitive towards Golden Indonesia 2045.
vision.mission1: Enhance connectivity and accessibility of transportation across all regions of Indonesia
vision.mission2: Drive digital and smart technology-based transportation transformation
vision.mission3: Build sustainable and environmentally friendly transportation infrastructure
vision.mission4: Improve transportation safety and security through effective regulation
vision.mission5: Develop competent and professional transportation human resources
vision.value1_desc: Prioritizing safety as the foundation of every transportation policy and operation
vision.value2_desc: Building green transportation by reducing carbon emissions and adopting renewable energy
vision.value3_desc: Encouraging the adoption of AI, IoT, and automation technologies in the national transportation system
vision.value4_desc: Providing equitable transportation access for all segments of Indonesian society
vision.strategic1: Construction of 2,500 km new railway lines including high-speed rail
vision.strategic2: Development of 45 international ports as global logistics hubs
vision.strategic3: Implementation of autonomous transportation systems in 15 metropolitan cities
vision.strategic4: Achievement of 30% electric vehicles target from total national fleet
vision.strategic5: Enhancement of inter-island connectivity through permanent bridges and tunnels

conclusion.title: Conclusion
conclusion.subtitle: Summary of findings and recommendations based on transportation statistical analysis
conclusion.finding1_text: National transportation passenger volume grew 105% over the past decade, from 14.44 billion (2021) to 29.60 billion (2031). Road transport dominates with 85.8% contribution to total passengers.
conclusion.finding2_text: Digital ticketing adoption reached 92% in 2031, up from 35% in 2021. AI-based transportation management systems have been implemented in 78% of major national terminals.
conclusion.finding3_text: Infrastructure growth index shows significant improvement: road network (+85%), railway network (+105%), and ports (+95%). Cumulative investment reached IDR 850 trillion.
conclusion.finding4_text: Electric vehicle adoption increased from 0.5% (2021) to 18% (2031) of total new vehicle registrations. The 30% target by 2035 still requires policy acceleration.
conclusion.rec1: Accelerate high-speed railway construction to connect major cities in Java and Sumatra
conclusion.rec2: Increase port infrastructure investment to support Indonesia's vision as a global maritime hub
conclusion.rec3: Accelerate electric vehicle subsidy and incentive programs to achieve 30% target by 2035
conclusion.rec4: Develop AI-based smart traffic management systems across all metropolitan cities
conclusion.rec5: Strengthen inter-island connectivity through permanent bridge and tunnel construction
conclusion.conclusion_text: Indonesia has demonstrated significant progress in the transportation sector over the past decade. Infrastructure growth and digital technology adoption have been the main drivers of improved transportation services. However, challenges such as inter-regional disparities, carbon emissions, and the need for sustainable investment still require serious attention. With proper policy implementation and strategic investment, Indonesia has the potential to become a transportation leader in the ASEAN region towards Golden Indonesia 2045.
